title: It's the End of the World as we know it = TV & Conservatives are DEAD

enrichment.summary: Kyle argues that TV is dying based on the aging Fox News audience and the collapse of video rental stores as an analogy, and predicts this will kill the Republican Party's influence within four years. He believes the shift to internet media will politically awaken conservative viewers, leading to an independent, YouTube-native presidential candidate and a social-democratic future. He makes these predictions in the context of the 2016 election cycle.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: TV is essentially dead, killed by aging audiences and internet bandwidth improvements
Dead or dying because it relies on TV-watching older voters who will either switch to internet or die off
A pure TV personality who wouldn't be viable in an internet-only media environment
Only conservative due to ignorance from TV; will 'wake up' once they shift to internet media
The next cycle will feature internet-native independent candidates who run via YouTube subscriber counts rather than poll numbers
Hopes he wins and gets a second term as part of a social-democratic future
